data.title: Kaldvík AS facing challenges with reduced salmon prices affecting outlook for Q1 2026 post Q3 results from 21 Nov 2025.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, it was reported that Kaldvik AS has faced challenges following the release of their Q3 2025 results on November 21, 2025. The company has noted a decline in their Q1 2026 projections due to various factors, including early harvest issues and external influences such as a drop in salmon prices.

data.title: U.S. Postal Service Board of Governors to hold public meeting on Feb. 5 at 475 L'Enfant Plaza SW in Washington, DC, starting at 3 p.m. ET.
data.description: On February 5, 2026, the U.S. Postal Service Board of Governors will convene for an open session at the Postal Service headquarters located at 475 L'Enfant Plaza SW in Washington, DC. The meeting is scheduled to commence at 3 p.m. Eastern Time and is open to the public.

data.title: Novel oral weight-loss drug by Structure Therapeutics CEO seeks to compete with Novo Nordisk, as oral therapies gain traction. Prepares for Phase 3 trials with aleniglipron. Spotlight on obesity treatments at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference.
data.description: Following the introduction of the first oral GLP-1 weight-loss drug by Novo Nordisk A/S (NVO) at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference, attention has shifted toward obesity treatments at the rapidly growing market. According to an interview at the conference, CEO Ray Stevens of Structure Therapeutics Inc (GPCR) outlined the company's strategy to establish itself as a prominent player in the oral therapies sector. Stevens mentioned that Structure intends to progress its daily oral GLP-1 candidate, aleniglipron, into Phase 3 trials within the upcoming year. With the anticipation of strong performances from recently approved and upcoming oral pills by Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly and Co. (LLY), the focus on obesity treatments is expected to intensify.

data.title: EquipmentShare, a construction technology leader, debuts on Nasdaq as "EQPT" after a decade of growth and innovation.
data.description: EquipmentShare, a renowned company specializing in advanced technology for construction sites, has commenced trading on the Nasdaq after enjoying ten years of remarkable expansion and groundbreaking advancements.

data.title: iCEV debuts better Custom Assessments tool for educators, Jan. 23, 2026, promising improved experience.
data.description: iCEV, a top provider of Career and Technical Education (CTE) solutions, revealed a new and improved method for educators in Lubbock, Texas, on Jan. 23, 2026. This system offers a more user-friendly and dependable approach for creating and handling CTE assessments. The enhanced Custom Assessments experience is aligned with instructional guidelines, providing educators with a streamlined process. This innovation aims to offer educators a more efficient way to manage assessments in the CTE field.

data.title: Dow drops 250 points post-Greenland news, Intel plummets after earnings. Stock market reaction covered by Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: On Friday, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline of 250 points after witnessing a rally due to the Greenland deal in the previous two days. Intel, in particular, saw a significant drop in its stock value following their earnings report. The stock market today was marked by this downturn, with investors closely monitoring the impact of recent events on the market.

data.title: Edgecore Networks in Taiwan commits to OpenWiFi Movement for open networking innovation, emphasizing full-stack, modern, and interoperable network architectures.
data.description: Edgecore Networks, a subsidiary of Accton Technology based in Hsinchu, Taiwan, has announced its dedication to advancing the OpenWiFi Movement. They are focusing on creating modern, open, and interoperable network solutions for enterprises, data centers, and telecommunication service providers. The company emphasized their progress in real-world evaluations and ongoing efforts to develop full-stack network architectures. This commitment comes as network environments become increasingly complex, with a push towards innovative solutions.

data.title: C&F Financial Corp appoints Dr. David H. Downs to its Board effective Feb 24, 2026. He has been on the C&F Central VA Advisory Board for 12 years.
data.description: In Toano, Virginia, on January 23, 2026, C&F Financial Corporation, the parent company of C&F Bank, revealed that Dr. David H. Downs will join as a director on February 24, 2026. Dr. Downs, a member of the C&F Central Virginia Advisory Board for more than 12 years, will also assume a directorial role at the Bank. The Corporation is listed on NASDAQ under the symbol CFFI.

data.title: Winter Storm Fern: Duke Energy prepares with 18,000+ workers from 27 states and Canada as it approaches Carolinas.
data.description: Duke Energy crews and support teams are finalizing preparations in Charlotte, North Carolina, as Winter Storm Fern is projected to hit the Carolinas this weekend. The storm is anticipated to bring challenging conditions, prompting Duke Energy to mobilize resources for potential power outages. Residents are advised to stay updated on the storm's progress and prepare for possible disruptions. For more information, visit the Duke Energy News Center for media resources including B-roll footage and high-resolution images.

data.title: Philip Morris seeks FDA approval for ZYN as safer tobacco product for adult smokers 21+.
data.description: A team from Philip Morris International (PMI) shared research with the FDA's Tobacco Products Scientific Advisory Committee (TPSAC) in Stamford, CT. PMI, listed on NYSE as PM, presented data for their ZYN nicotine pouch products for a Modified Risk Tobacco Product (MRTP) certification. This designation would enable PMI's U.S. branches to inform adult smokers over 21 that switching to their products could be beneficial.
